example.userinput: bears are at the door, did you leave the garbage out?

score_reasons.reason: Let the Malayalam word have English letters like "karadi"
Threw in conjugations, particles, plural കൾ without checking if you already know those. Beginner can mean "I know the alphabet" or "I know 100 words"

assistant_output.intro: Your English sentence: "Bears are at the door, did you leave the garbage out?" This is actually 2 parts. Let's tackle them one at a time.

part.title: Bears are at the door
part.sentence_structure: [Noun - subject] [Noun - location] [Location word] [Verb - "to be" present] Malayalam often puts the verb at the end, and location comes before the verb.

word.english: to put / to keep / to leave

clues.clue: "Bears" is plural. In Malayalam, you often add കൾ to make a noun plural.
"At the door" = door + a location particle. You need to figure out which particle means "at/on/near".
"Are" comes from the verb ഉണ്ടാകുക. For "there are bears", you'd use the present tense form. Think: how do you say "is/are there"?

part.title: Did you leave the garbage out?

clues.clue: "Did you leave" = past tense of വെക്കുക. You'll need to conjugate it for past + "you".
"Out" = പുറത്ത്. In Malayalam, "leave out" becomes "പുറത്ത് വെക്കുക".
To make a question in Malayalam, you usually add a question particle at the end. It's for yes/no questions, not "wh-" questions.

example.userinput: bears are at the door, did you leave the garbage out?

score_reasons.reason: Doesn't provide verbose text at the start
Gives English abbreviations beside Malayalam words
Vocabulary table needs to be improved by proper separation

assistant_output.header: Your Sentence: "Bears are at the door, did you leave the garbage out?"

sentence_structure.clause: Subject + Object + Verb (past tense, question form)

clues.clue: Malayalam is SOV (Subject → Object → Verb) — the verb comes at the end
"Bears" is plural — think about how to make കരടി plural!
For "at the door", you'll need a location particle after വാതിൽ
The second sentence is a past tense question — Malayalam questions often use the suffix "ഓ" at the end
"You" in Malayalam is നീ (informal) or താങ്കൾ (formal)
